verdict: All five technical falsification gates pass within their stated local evidence boundaries. This does not prove broad portability, maintainer-confirmed bug status, customer demand, or a business.

gates.name: Fault detection
gates.evidence: 20/20 seeded mutants detected across WebSocket, SSE, and REST synthetic fixtures.
gates.boundary: Disclosed seeded mutants across three purpose-built transport fixtures.

gates.name: Replay stability
gates.evidence: 20/20 synthetic minimized failures and 5/5 Etherpad minimized replays reproduced.
gates.boundary: Local Chromium and one machine; not a cross-platform flake estimate.

gates.name: Fourth-app integration
gates.evidence: Unmodified Etherpad 3.3.2 integrated end to end in 35.1 minutes.
gates.boundary: One unfamiliar production application under a local default configuration.

gates.name: PR runtime
gates.evidence: 50 schedules, three clients, four workers completed in 11.95 s (47 passed, 3 failed, 0 errors).
gates.boundary: Synthetic WebSocket fixture campaign, local four-worker measurement.

gates.name: Novel actionable failure
gates.evidence: Etherpad reconnect UI-readiness failure shrank from 8 to 3 steps and replayed 5/5; targeted upstream source-test review found no equivalent real-network reconnect-then-type case.
gates.boundary: Actionable candidate, not a maintainer-confirmed upstream bug; source-test search was targeted rather than exhaustive.

target.name: Etherpad
target.configuration: official source build, default local rusty.db, anonymous public pads

integrationEffort.includes: upstream selection, source clone, dependency install, application build, adapter implementation, tests, campaign, shrinking, replay, and source-test audit

failures.message: issued append "[[rf-73000-2]]" had no immediate editor effect

client-1.document:
Welcome to Etherpad!


This pad text is synchronized as you type, so that everyone viewing this page sees the same text. This allows you to collaborate seamlessly on documents!


Etherpad on Github: https://github.com/ether/etherpad[[rf-73000-1]][[rf-73000-0]]

sourceTestAudit.scope: targeted grep and review of Etherpad 3.3.2 frontend-new, frontend, and backend tests
relevantExistingCoverage: unaccepted_commit_warning.spec.ts checks commit acknowledgement and a simulated pending-commit disconnect warning
pad_modal.js checks that a simulated disconnected state disables the editor
xxauto_reconnect.js checks timer-driven reconnect reload behavior
padHelper.ts waits for initial contenteditable readiness and documents silently dropped input during initial load
sourceTestAudit.boundary: This is a targeted source-test comparison, not an exhaustive coverage proof; maintainer confirmation is still required.

publication.npmBoundary: The supplied token authenticated, but npm required an interactive browser authentication/2FA step. No npm version was published.

remainingClaimsNotProven: broad portability across several unrelated production applications
maintainer confirmation that the Etherpad finding is an upstream defect
customer demand, pricing, or commercial viability
trademark or legal clearance for the ReplicaFuzz name
